archive.title: Harvey Secures $200M at $11B Valuation to Expand AI Legal Agents Platform
archive.summary: Harvey raised $200M bringing its valuation to $11B and total capital to $1B. Co-led by GIC and Sequoia, the funding will expand Harvey's agentic AI platform including Spectre, an autonomous system that can handle entire client matters. Harvey now partners with 100+ top US law firms and 500+ in-house teams.

archive.title: AI Built for Law Outperforms ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini on Legal Reasoning Benchmark
archive.summary: Descrybe launched DescrybeLM, an AI system built specifically for legal reasoning. On 200 multistate bar exam questions, DescrybeLM answered all 200 correctly while ChatGPT 5.2, Claude Opus 4.5, and Gemini 3 Pro each missed 13-23 questions — showing purpose-built legal AI outperforming general models.

archive.title: SpotDraft Reports 100% YoY Customer Growth, Processes Over 1M Contracts Annually
archive.summary: SpotDraft reported 100% year-on-year growth in its customer base with contract volumes up 173%. The platform now serves 50,000 monthly active users processing over one million contracts annually. Its privacy-first on-device AI runs entirely on Snapdragon processors.

archive.title: HSBC Selects Harvey AI as Enterprise Legal AI Platform
archive.summary: HSBC selected Harvey AI as its enterprise legal AI platform, deploying it across the bank's global legal operations. The deal signals growing adoption of legal AI by major financial institutions beyond traditional law firm clients.

archive.title: Legora Hits $100M ARR, Opens Munich Office as Legal AI Race Intensifies
archive.summary: Legora announced hitting $100M ARR in just 18 months, opened offices in Munich, Houston, and Chicago, and plans to grow to 300+ US employees by year-end. The platform emphasizes collaborative workspaces with deep integration into iManage and NetDocuments for BigLaw.

archive.title: Why Native Legal AI Is Required for India
archive.summary: Artificial Lawyer examines why India's legal system needs AI tools built specifically for Indian law — arguing that global platforms like Harvey and CoCounsel lack the jurisdictional depth, multi-language support, and understanding of India-specific regulatory frameworks required for meaningful adoption.

archive.title: Charting Change in Legal: The Realities of AI Adoption, and an Inflection Point
archive.summary: Legal IT Insider reports the legal industry has reached a genuine inflection point — less fear, more pragmatism. Corporate legal AI adoption jumped from 23% to 52% in one year, with 64% of in-house teams expecting to depend less on outside counsel due to AI capabilities they are building internally.

archive.title: AUTOMATIC Partners with Law.co to Deliver Agentic AI Infrastructure for Law Firms
archive.summary: AUTOMATIC announced a strategic partnership with Law.co to deliver autonomous AI agents for law firms — enabling legal research, contract workflows, and client intake without sacrificing compliance. The partnership signals the shift from AI assistants to autonomous agentic infrastructure in legal practice.

archive.title: Legal Tech Sees Second-Busiest Funding Quarter on Record in Q1 2026
archive.summary: Legal-tech startups recorded their second-highest quarterly VC intake in Q1 2026. Legaltech funding hit $4.3B across 356 deals in the trailing twelve months, with AI-powered legal tools driving 70% of investment. Harvey and Legora accounted for nearly half of all Q1 funding.

archive.title: Microsoft Copilot Labeled 'For Entertainment Purposes Only' in Terms of Service — Sparks Legal Debate
archive.summary: Microsoft's terms of use describe Copilot as 'for entertainment purposes only', contradicting its enterprise positioning at $30/user/month. Microsoft called it 'legacy language' from when Copilot launched as a Bing companion, promising an update. The disclaimer raises questions about legal recourse if business decisions are based on incorrect Copilot outputs.

archive.title: Legora Reaches $5.55B Valuation as AI Legal Tech Boom Endures
archive.summary: Legora closed a $550M Series D at $5.55B valuation, hit $100M ARR, opened Munich and Houston offices, and plans to grow to 300+ US employees by year-end. The platform emphasizes collaborative workspaces with deep integration into iManage and NetDocuments.

archive.title: How Law Firms Can Lead the Agentic AI Era — And What Clients Now Expect
archive.summary: Harvard Law School Forum on Corporate Governance examines how agentic AI is shifting client expectations — law firms are now expected to deploy autonomous AI systems for document review, contract negotiation, and research rather than billing associates for tasks AI can execute faster and cheaper.

archive.title: HSBC Announces Harvey AI as Enterprise Legal AI Platform
archive.summary: HSBC selected Harvey AI as its enterprise legal AI platform, deploying it across the bank's global legal operations. The deal signals growing adoption of legal AI by major financial institutions beyond traditional law firm clients.

archive.title: Microsoft Copilot Cowork — Anthropic's Claude Powers New Agentic AI Agent Across M365
archive.summary: Microsoft unveiled Copilot Cowork, built in partnership with Anthropic, embedding Claude's long-running agentic capabilities into M365. The system handles multi-step tasks across Word, Teams, and Outlook — observable, steerable, and governed within Microsoft's enterprise security framework. Currently in research preview via the Frontier program.

archive.title: Microsoft 365 E7: New $99/User AI Bundle Launches May 1 — Copilot, Agents, and Advanced Security
archive.summary: Microsoft announced M365 E7 at $99/user/month — its most expensive commercial tier — combining M365 E5, Microsoft 365 Copilot, Agent 365, and Work IQ with Microsoft Entra Suite and advanced Defender, Intune, and Purview security capabilities, launching May 1.

archive.title: Fortune: Microsoft's Copilot Cowork and E7 Signal the End of the AI Chatbot Era
archive.summary: Fortune examines how Microsoft's dual announcements — Copilot Cowork (built on Claude) and the E7 license — represent a strategic shift from AI as productivity add-on to AI as autonomous worker infrastructure, with implications for enterprise software vendors and legal AI platforms alike.

archive.title: Reworked: Microsoft Launches Copilot Cowork, Built on Claude, to Execute Long-Running M365 Tasks
archive.summary: A technical look at how Copilot Cowork's architecture — multi-step reasoning, cross-app task execution, and transparent progress tracking — differs fundamentally from existing Copilot chat features and what it means for enterprise digital workplace and legal workflow strategy.

archive.title: Microsoft M365 Blog: Powering Frontier Transformation with Copilot and Agents
archive.summary: Microsoft's official blog outlines its multi-model philosophy for Copilot Cowork — choosing the best model for each task regardless of who built it — with Anthropic's Claude highlighted for long-running agentic work and Harvey deeply integrated for legal and professional services workflows.

archive.title: MSDynamicsWorld: M365 Copilot Updates Extend Cowork to Business Apps Alongside E7 Launch
archive.summary: Beyond knowledge-worker workflows, Microsoft's March 9 announcements also extend Copilot agentic capabilities into Dynamics 365 business apps — broadening Cowork's footprint into operations, finance, and supply chain functions, positioned as core infrastructure rather than a productivity add-on.

archive.title: The Outrageous Price of Legal AI: Why Harvey and Legora's Tactics Reveal a Deeper Problem
archive.summary: Purple Law argues Harvey and Legora's pricing is unsustainably high relative to value delivered — and that Microsoft's Copilot Cowork and Anthropic's direct legal plugins are accelerating pressure on mid-market legal AI vendors to justify their premium over foundation model alternatives.

archive.title: Claude vs Gemini for Lawyers: Which AI Is Better for Legal Work in 2026?
archive.summary: A practitioner-focused comparison of Claude and Gemini for legal work: Claude favoured for contract drafting, document analysis, and jurisdictional precision; Gemini preferred for legal research requiring real-time regulatory monitoring and current statutory text. Both are pulling ahead of ChatGPT in legal deployment.

archive.title: The Rise of Agentic AI: Transforming Legal and Enterprise Workflows
archive.summary: As Microsoft's Copilot Cowork goes live, a broader analysis of how agentic AI — systems that plan, execute, and reason across multi-step legal workflows — is shifting from experimentation to deployment, and what governance frameworks firms need before delegating consequential tasks to AI agents.

archive.title: Top 5 Alternatives to Harvey AI and Legora in 2026
archive.summary: Purple Law reviews the leading alternatives to Harvey and Legora — including Anthropic's Claude (via Cowork), CoCounsel, Spellbook, and emerging Indian platforms — as the legal AI market fragments between premium vertical tools and powerful foundation model options.

archive.title: Anthropic Sues Trump Administration Over Pentagon 'Supply Chain Risk' Designation
archive.summary: Anthropic filed two federal lawsuits after the Pentagon labeled it a 'supply-chain risk to national security' — a designation historically reserved for foreign adversaries. The dispute stems from Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei's insistence on guardrails against autonomous weapons use.

archive.title: Legalweek 2026 Opens at Javits Center — 6,000+ Attendees, AI Dominates Agenda
archive.summary: Legalweek opens at the Javits Center in New York (Mar 9–12) with 6,000+ attendees from 50 countries. Bloomberg Law unveils a 'Deep Thinking' mode for its AI Assistant, while panels cover agentic workflows, responsible adoption, and vendor–firm partnerships.

archive.title: Legal Tech Vendors Roll Out Wave of Product Launches Ahead of Legalweek
archive.summary: DISCO unveiled an all-inclusive platform bundling e-discovery and Cecilia AI at one price. iManage enhanced Insight+ with enterprise-scale metadata indexing. Reveal, Monjur, ChronoTracer, and ALIGN also announced new capabilities.

archive.title: State AI Legislation Wave: Oregon, Utah, Washington, and Arizona Pass Bills in Single Week
archive.summary: Oregon passed a chatbot disclosure bill, Utah enacted a provenance bill, Washington is on the cusp of passing both chatbot and provenance bills, and Arizona's Senate approved a provenance measure — a landmark week for US state-level AI regulation.

archive.title: Clarra to Present Legalweek Panel on Agentic AI for Complex Litigation
archive.summary: Litigation analytics platform Clarra will present a featured session at Legalweek on using agentic and generative AI for complex litigation — exploring how autonomous AI workflows can accelerate case strategy and document review.

archive.title: AI Adoption Among Legal Professionals Has More Than Doubled in One Year
archive.summary: A new 8am Report finds nearly 70% of legal professionals now use generative AI for work — more than double from a year ago. However, most law firms still lack formal AI policies or training, creating a gap between individual adoption and institutional readiness.

archive.title: GPT-5.4 Is OpenAI's Most Factual Model Yet — Harvey Reports 91% on BigLaw Bench
archive.summary: OpenAI shipped GPT-5.4 on March 5 with 33% fewer hallucinations than GPT-5.2. Harvey confirmed a 91% score on its proprietary BigLaw Bench for document-heavy legal work — the highest by any model to date.

archive.title: Contract Law in the Age of Agentic AI: Who's Really Clicking 'Accept'?
archive.summary: Proskauer asks whether agentic AI tools that act on behalf of users qualify as 'electronic agents' forming enforceable contracts under existing law — a critical open question as zero-touch contracting becomes mainstream.

archive.title: Ivo Opens London and New York Offices to Expand Legal AI Footprint
archive.summary: San Francisco-based legal AI startup Ivo announced expansion into London and New York to accelerate adoption among Magic Circle and BigLaw firms.

archive.title: Bloomberg Law Showcases 'Deep Thinking' AI Mode and Future-Ready Solutions at Legalweek
archive.summary: Bloomberg Law debuted a 'Deep Thinking' reasoning mode for its AI Assistant at Legalweek, alongside panels on generative AI's impact on legal workflows and how firms can build future-ready tech stacks.

archive.title: Claude Overtakes ChatGPT on App Store as Users Boycott Over Pentagon Deal
archive.summary: Anthropic's Claude climbed to No. 1 on Apple's App Store, dethroning ChatGPT days after OpenAI announced a nine-figure deal with the U.S. Department of War. The shift has implications for legal AI vendors choosing their underlying model provider.

archive.title: March 2026 AI Launch Wave: What Lawyers Should Make of GPT-5.4, Claude 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro
archive.summary: A breakdown of the wave of AI model releases this month — GPT-5.4, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Grok 4.20, and Chinese challengers GLM-5 and MiniMax M2.5. The frontier is shifting from chat quality to agentic execution and document-heavy work.

archive.title: The Agentic AI Revolution: Managing Legal Risks
archive.summary: Squire Patton Boggs examines liability gaps and governance challenges when agentic AI systems autonomously plan and execute multi-step legal workflows — covering accountability, error handling, and risk frameworks for firms.

archive.title: ContractPodAi: Agentic AI Transforms Legal and Enterprise Workflows
archive.summary: Zero-touch contracting for low-risk agreements, AI-generated negotiation playbooks with 95% first-pass accuracy, and Ironclad, DocuSign, and Icertis racing to embed autonomous capabilities into their CLM platforms.

archive.title: EU AI Act and Colorado AI Act Deadlines Loom — Compliance Now Mandatory
archive.summary: EU AI Act (August 2026) and Colorado AI Act (June) make formalized AI policies a compliance obligation. Gartner forecasts 40% of agentic AI projects may face cancellation by 2027 due to costs and unclear ROI.

archive.title: Harvey Becomes US Open's First 'Official Legal Assistant' in Multiyear Deal
archive.summary: Harvey and the USTA announced a multiyear sponsorship making Harvey the US Open's first 'Official Legal Assistant' — Harvey's first sports deal, signalling legal AI companies shifting toward mainstream brand-building.

archive.title: Anthropic's Claude Legal Plugin — Legal IT Insider's Full Analysis
archive.summary: Legal IT Insider's deep-dive analysis of Anthropic's legal plugin for Claude, examining whether foundation-model providers entering legal vertical software marks a structural threat to incumbents or market overreaction.

archive.title: The Legal AI Split That Will Reshape Every Law Firm's Tech Stack
archive.summary: Lawfuel argues Harvey and Legora face increasingly difficult positioning — caught between incumbents with authoritative databases, foundation model companies with scale advantages, and Thomson Reuters' embedded CoCounsel ecosystem.

archive.title: Husch Blackwell Rolls Out Legora Firm-Wide; Harvey Makes Major Hires; DeepIP Raises $25M
archive.summary: US firm Husch Blackwell deployed Legora across all practice groups. Harvey hired former Ashurst CDO Tara Waters and ex-Marsh McLennan counsel Farrah Pepper. IP analytics startup DeepIP raised $25M.

archive.title: Harvey Integrates Legal AI Agents Into Microsoft 365 Copilot
archive.summary: Harvey announced a deep integration with Microsoft 365 Copilot, letting lawyers invoke Harvey's AI agents directly inside Word, Outlook, and Teams via @Harvey mentions.

archive.title: Legal AI Is Splitting in Two — And Most People Miss the Difference
archive.summary: Fortune argues legal AI is diverging into authoritative tools (CoCounsel, Westlaw) versus general-purpose AI agents like Anthropic's Claude.

archive.title: Intapp Strikes Deals with Anthropic and Harvey
archive.summary: Intapp partnered with both Anthropic and Harvey, using Claude models for AI agents handling conflict checks and client intelligence.

archive.title: Lawyer Uses Claude Skills, Legal World Loses It
archive.summary: A US lawyer's viral post described running his firm as a 'Claude-native' practice — using Anthropic's Skills for contract drafting and due diligence, sparking debate about foundation models' readiness for legal work.

archive.title: SpotDraft Showcases VerifAI at India AI Impact Summit 2026
archive.summary: SpotDraft demonstrated its on-device contract AI tool VerifAI at the India AI Impact Summit, pitching privacy-first contract automation to enterprise legal teams.

archive.title: Linklaters Creates Dedicated Team of 'AI Lawyers'
archive.summary: Linklaters formed a specialized team advising lawyers firm-wide on AI deployment, complementing its chatbot 'Laila' and Microsoft Copilot rollout.

archive.title: CoCounsel Now Available to One Million Professionals Worldwide
archive.summary: Thomson Reuters announced CoCounsel has crossed the one-million-user mark across 107 countries.

archive.title: Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas Embarks on an AI-First Future
archive.summary: India's largest law firm selected Legora as its enterprise GenAI platform alongside Harvey on pilot and Lucio for specific use cases.

archive.title: Trilegal Partners with Lucio AI for Knowledge Management and Document Review
archive.summary: Trilegal embedded Lucio's generative AI tools across knowledge-management and document-review workflows, hosted on the firm's own Azure environment.

archive.title: SpotDraft Secures $8M from Qualcomm Ventures, Valuation Nears $380M
archive.summary: SpotDraft raised $8M from Qualcomm Ventures to expand on-device AI, nearly doubling its valuation to $380M.

archive.title: Anthropic Unveils Claude Legal Plugin, Causes Market Meltdown
archive.summary: Anthropic launched legal-specific plugins for Claude bundling contract review, document drafting, and research skills — triggering a selloff in Thomson Reuters and RELX stock.
